title: US Navy support for NASCAR 2026 San Diego Weekend

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: US Navy support for NASCAR 2026 San Diego Weekend
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All documents pertaining to collaboration between the US Navy and the TV production of NASCAR 2026 San Diego Weekend. Such documents may include but should not be limited to: script notes; production assistance agreements; photographs; and all records of communications between employees of the Navy and members of the cast, crew, and production company of NASCAR 2026 San Diego Weekend, including memos, notes of meetings and conference calls, emails, and other forms of communication.

NASCAR 2026 San Diego Weekend appears (as "NASCAR 2026 San Diego Weekend [Amazon Prime streaming, FS1 network and CW network]") in a list of Navy-supported productions for the year 2025 that was released to me through FOIA. That list is attached to this request.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: Grievance complaint outcomes
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All written determinations, decision letters, findings or resolutions issued in response to grievance complaints in the Detroit Continuum of Care system from Jan. 1, 2025 to present.

All records reflecting determinations where a grievance was substantiated, in whole or in part, due to a provider’s or agency’s failure to comply with grievance investigation procedures.

All records related to oversight of grievance handling by providers, including:

- Monitoring reports, audits or compliance reviews;
- Corrective action plans;
- Performance evaluations referencing grievance handling, complaint resolution, or coordinated entry compliance.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Aaron Mondry

communications.subject: Fw: [EXTERNAL] Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: Grievance complaint outcomes
communications.communication:
This email serves as the City of Detroit’s response to the above-referenced matter. Your request was received at the City of Detroit Law Department Freedom of Information Act Section via facsimile or email on May 4, 2026. Because your request was received by electronic transmission, pursuant to Section 5(1) of the Michigan Freedom of Information Act (the “Act”), MCL 15.235(1); it is deemed to have been received at the Law Department on the next business day, May 5, 2026.

Pursuant to Section 5(2) of the Act, MCL 15.235(2), the City’s response is due within five (5) business days. However, due to the nature and the scope of your request and the volume of the requests received by the City, we are extending the City’s response deadline by ten (10) additional business days in accordance with Section 5(2) (d) of the Act, MCL 15.235(2) (d). Therefore, your request will be granted, denied, or granted in part and denied in part on or before May 26, 2026.

Your request is being handled by Devin Farrelly. If you have questions regarding your request, or if you did not provide an email address in your request, please forward it to Devin Farrelly at farrellyd@detroitmi.gov<mailto:farrellyd@detroitmi.gov> to provide you a response more readily than by regular mail or fax. Mail and fax are not preferred at this time since they both require in-office support. We thank you in advance for your understanding.
